title: Today's AI Brief: The Download: AI hacking beyond Mythos, and chatbots’ impact on our brains
summary: A look at AI hacking and chatbot impacts on our brains

imageCaption: The Download: AI hacking beyond Mythos, and chatbots’ impact on our brains
imageSourceTitle: The Download: AI hacking beyond Mythos, and chatbots’ impact on our brains

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and this is your AI Brief. Let's dive into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, there's been a significant event in AI security. A hack at Meta, reportedly, has shown that there's more to AI security than just Mythos. This incident is making waves because it highlights the vulnerabilities in AI systems, even those as robust as Mythos. It's a reminder that as AI becomes more integral to our lives, securing it is becoming increasingly complex.
A $30B Bet on AI Data Centers

Next, AirTrunk is committing a whopping $30 billion to build 5GW of AI data centers in India by 2030. This massive investment is part of a larger trend where technology and infrastructure groups are expanding their computing capabilities. It's a big move, and India is clearly becoming a key player in the global AI infrastructure landscape. The scale of this investment is a testament to the growing demand for AI computing power.

The impact of chatbots on our brains is another fascinating topic. As these AI systems become more prevalent, researchers are studying how they affect our thinking and behavior. It's an area that's both intriguing and a bit unsettling, as we begin to realize just how much these chatbots are influencing our daily interactions. From how we shop to how we communicate, chatbots are changing the game.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
